name: COM Hijacking With Suspicious Locations
notes:
This rule detects potential COM hijacking where the "Server" (In/Out) is pointing to a suspicious location.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: COM Hijacking For Persistence With Suspicious Locations
SIGMA Description: Detects potential COM object hijacking where the "Server" (In/Out) is pointing to a supsicious or unsuale location


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\clsid\%' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\inprocserver32\%' OR LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\localserver32\%' AQL filter query

when the event matches LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\users\public\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\desktop\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\appdata\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\downloads\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\windows\temp\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\start menu\programs\startup\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\system32\spool\drivers\color\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%temp%%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%tmp%%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%appdata%%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY COM Hijacking With Suspicious Locations on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\clsid\%' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\inprocserver32\%' OR LOWER("Registry Key") LIKE '%\localserver32\%' AQL filter query

AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\users\public\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\desktop\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\appdata\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\downloads\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\windows\temp\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\start menu\programs\startup\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%\system32\spool\drivers\color\%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%temp%%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%tmp%%' or LOWER("Registry Value Data") LIKE '%%appdata%%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: COM Hijacking With Suspicious Locations
Event description: Detects potential COM hijacking where the "Server" (In/Out) is pointing to a suspicious location.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
